summary: Bug fix verification for mobile registration consent blocker. Fixed React hooks violation in AIAssistant.js. All 6 test cases passed.
passed_tests: AI Assistant button does NOT exist on /register page - BUG FIXED
AI Assistant button does NOT exist on /login page - BUG FIXED
Consent checkbox is visible on mobile (24x24px - h-6 w-6)
Registration form shows error 'You must accept the data processing terms to continue' when submitting without consent
Registration succeeds when consent is checked - redirects to /login with success toast
Backend returns 400 error 'Analytics consent is required for customer registration' when analytics_consent is false
AI Assistant IS visible on homepage (non-auth page) - working correctly
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: React hooks violation in AIAssistant.js - moved early return AFTER all useState/useRef/useEffect hooks to comply with React rules of hooks
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix verified. AIAssistant.js had a React hooks violation where early return was placed before useState hooks. Fixed by moving all hooks before the conditional return. All consent-related features working correctly on mobile viewport (375x812).
rca_of_the_issue: Root Cause: The previous fix added 'if (isAuthPage) return null;' at line 166, BEFORE the useState hooks (lines 170-183). This violates React's rules of hooks which require hooks to be called in the same order on every render. Fix: Moved all useState, useRef, useCallback, and useEffect hooks BEFORE the conditional return statement.
